data.description:
## Why
Two audited tests assert on source text for negative-space/comment properties that have no
runtime behavior to exercise: `test_det_invariants.py::test_verify_command_lint_documents_
shellcheck_prior_art` (`inspect.getsource(det_floor)` asserts the word "shellcheck" appears
in a code comment; `:333-334`) and `test_llm_timeouts.py::test_no_total_runtime_timeout_
mechanism` (asserts `signal.alarm`/`Timer(` absent and one exact `asyncio.run(...)` string;
`:325-330`). Both have genuinely-behavioral siblings already (the real `test_verify_command_
lint`; the read-timeout/tool-timeout liveness probes). VALIDATED.

## What
For each: delete if fully subsumed by its behavioral sibling, OR relabel as an explicitly-
supplemental structural/doc guard and loosen the brittle exact-string assertions — never
counted as behavioral coverage.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/test_det_invariants.py`, `tests/unit/test_llm_timeouts.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The shellcheck-prior-art test is deleted or documented as a comment-presence guard
      only; the real lint behavior remains covered by its sibling.
- [ ] The no-total-timeout test drops the exact `asyncio.run(...)` string assertion; the
      liveness property is anchored on the behavioral timeout probes.
- [ ] Neither is counted as behavioral coverage in any coverage inventory.
- [ ] `make test` green.
data.title: Resolve the two negative-space source-string guards (shellcheck note, no-total-timeout)
